original: Space Shuttle Endeavour (STS-69) launches into orbit

Jimmy Page,Robert Plant,John Paul Jones, andJohn Bonhammake their live debut as Led Zeppelin (billed as The New Yardbirds) before about 1,200 students at Egegård School's Teen Club in Gladsaxe, Denmark
Johnny Gruelle patents his Raggedy Ann doll (US Patent D47789)
Entertainment and Sports Programming Network (ESPN) launches; the first show is SportsCenter
Discovery of the earliest evidence of surgery from a 31,000-year-old skeleton with an amputated lower leg in a cave in East Kalimantan, Borneo, published in "Nature"
Eric Hill, British children's books author and illustrator (Where's Spot), born in Holloway, London (d. 2014)
Drew Weissman, American scientist (2023 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for mRNA vaccine technology), born in Lexington, Massachusetts[1]
Japan wins the men's gymnastics team combined exercises gold for the first of five consecutive Olympics at the Rome Games, beating the Soviet Union by just 2.5 points
Indian Space Agency loses contact with its Chandrayaan-2 moon lander just 2 km from landing on the Moon's surface
British archaeologists announce the discovery of a Neolithic "superhenge" under three feet of earth at Durrington Walls, a large ritual site near Stonehenge
Donald Henderson, American epidemiologist, led world campaign that eradicated smallpox, born in Lakewood, Ohio (d. 2016)
Philo Farnsworth demonstrates the first use of his electronic television in San Francisco
James Van Allen, American physicist (discovered Van Allen radiation belts), born in Mount Pleasant, Iowa (d. 2006)
Pedro I, son of King John VI, declares Brazil's independence from Portugal (Independence Day)

American weightlifter Charles Vinci wins his second consecutive bantamweight gold medal at the Rome Olympics with a world-record-equaling three-lift (snatch, clean & jerk, overhead press) total of 345.0 kilograms
Drury Gallagher wins the Manhattan Island Marathon Swim in an unofficial world record time of 6:41:35
Jingyi Le, Ying Shan, Ying Le, and Bin Lu swim the 4x100 freestyle in 3:37.91
In Australia, the entire nation observes a "day of humiliation" and prays for rain as a terrible drought kills livestock and threatens crops; rain begins to fall on September 10
Michael DeBakey, Lebanese-American cardiac surgeon and artificial heart pioneer, born in Lake Charles, Louisiana (d. 2008)
Strongest hurricane of the century in the Netherlands (wind force 12)
Bell X-2 sets an unofficial manned aircraft altitude record of 126,000 feet (38,404.8 meters)
Outfielder Don Grate of Tennessee throws a baseball a record 434 ft 1 in (132.3 m)
August Kekule von Stradonitz, German chemist who discovered structure of benzene ring, born in Darmstadt, rand Duchy of Hesse by the Rhine, German Confederation (d. 1896)
Heinrich David Stölzel, German horn player who developed the first valve for brass instruments, born in Schneeberg, Electrorate of Saxony, Holy Roman Empire (d. 1844)
Hurricane Ivan, a Category 5 hurricane, hits Grenada, killing 39 people and damaging 90% of its buildings
An 8.2 magnitude earthquake hits southwest of Pijijiapan, Mexico, killing at least 90 people; it is the strongest Mexican quake in a century
Asteroid 2014 RC makes a close approach to Earth (39,900 km; 24,800 mi)
Rashid Khantakes the first hat-trick in the history of the Caribbean Premier League
Don Messick, American cartoon voice actor (The Flintstones - "Bamm-Bamm"; The Jetsons - "Astro"; Scooby-Doo), born in Buffalo, New York (d. 1997)

English pirate Henry Every and his crew aboard the Fancy capture the armed Mughal trading ship Ganj-i-Sawai in the Arabian Sea in one of the most profitable raids in history, with loot valued between £325,000 and £600,000. The East India Company later compensates for the loss after Mughal EmperorAurangzebcloses five ports to English trade.

Alberto Ascariof Italy, driving a Ferrari, wins the Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship with a victory in the season-ending Italian Grand Prix at Monza, securing the title by 12 points over his countryman Giuseppe Farina
Future world professional cycling road race champion Hennie Kuiper of the Netherlands wins the road race gold medal at the Munich Olympics in 4:14:37
El Salvador becomes the first country to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender
Solar eclipse on this date might indicate the birth of the legendary Heracles in Thebes, Greece
The last surviving member of the marsupial thylacine species, Benjamin, dies alone in his cage at Hobart Zoo, Hobart, Tasmania, Australia

James Corbett knocks out 4 to 1 favorite and defending championJohn L. Sullivanin 21 rounds at the Olympic Club, New Orleans, for the world heavyweight boxing title; Sullivan's only defeat and his last fight
Edith Eleanor McLean is the first baby placed in an incubator at State Emigrant Hospital on Ward's Island, New York
Luigi Galvani, Italian anatomist and physicist ("animal electricity"), born in Bologna, Italy (d. 1798)
William Friese-Greene, British photographer and inventor (motion pictures), born in Bristol, England (d. 1921)
Olive Oatman, American (as a teen captured and released by Native Americans), born in La Harpe, Illinois (d. 1903)
{Walter) "Sonny" Rollins, American Grammy Ward-winning jazz saxophonist ("Blue Room"), environmentalist, and last survivor of iconic "A Great Fay in Harlem" photograph, born in New York City[1]
Gloria Gaynor [Fowles], American disco singer ("I Will Survive"; "I Am What I Am"), born in Newark, New Jersey
Julie Kavner, American actress and voice artist (Brenda-Rhoda, Marge-Simpsons), born in Los Angeles, California
Wildfires burn a record 2 million acres in California during the 2020 fire season, more than the state of Delaware, according to Cal Fire
Intense flooding caused by the heaviest rainfall in 140 years in Hong Kong, Shenzhen, and southern Chinese cities turns streets into rivers
Cyclone Yagi, Asia's most powerful storm of the year, makes landfall on Hainan, China, before striking Northern Vietnam with winds of 230 km per hour (140 mph), killing at least 127 people
Brigham Young University quarterback Ty Detmer passes NCAA record 11,606 yards
Boulder Dam (nowHooverDam) on the Colorado River between Nevada and Arizona begins operation
Scottish Matra-Ford driverJackie Stewartwins the Italian Grand Prix at Monza to secure his first Formula 1 World Drivers' Championship and his sixth Formula 1 win of the season
Dan Marinothrows his 100th career touchdown pass, becoming the fastest QB in NFL history to do so
Diane Warren, American songwriter (Toni Braxton - "Un-break My Heart"; Cher - "If I Could Turn Back Time"; DeBarge - "Rhythm of the Night"), born in Van Nuys, California
LeRoi Moore, American saxophonist and songwriter (Dave Matthews Band - "Stay (Wasting Time)"), born in Durham, North Carolina (d. 2008)
Jacob Lawrence, African-American artist (Sanitarium), born in Atlantic City, New Jersey (d. 2000)
Integration begins in Washington, D.C., and Baltimore, Maryland, public schools
The Cleveland Browns become the first team in NFL history to have a play reviewed by instant replay, Chicago 41, Browns 31
First test flight of the USAF F-22 Raptor takes place
"Uncle Sam" is first used to refer to the US by Troy Post of New York
City of Boston, Massachusetts, is founded
Third Crusade:Richard Iof England defeatsSaladinin the Battle of Arsuf
John Cornforth, Australian-British chemist (Nobel Prize 1975), born in Sydney, Australia (d. 2013)
